summary_one_line: A well-formed IPv6 datagram whose destination matches a configured unicast address, so receive==true and the non-reassembled receive path is taken.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An IPv6 header whose destination address equals one of the node's configured unicast addresses so that HasUnicastAddress(header.GetDestination()) is true at L1223, setting receive=true and driving execution into the `if (receive)` block where the `if (!aIsReassembled)` blocker at L1267 is evaluated and its false (non-reassembled) side is taken. Concretely the winner inputs carry a concentrated, structurally-valid header prefix (e.g. bytes 78 66 10 ... and the ff ff fe fc / fd de ad 00 be ef address pattern) that the parser accepts and that matches a node address.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving cmplog seeds share a tight 16-byte header prefix (0000: 78 66 10 ... ff ff fe fc, 0010: 03 a0 07 08 ... fd de ad 00 be ef) and at L1223 the winner takes the unicast-address-match side T=70 F=0, so receive becomes true and the blocker's false side is reached (resolved=10/10).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking naive seeds carry a different, less-structured prefix (db 62 5c ... and long 44 44 44 / 30 30 runs) and at L1223 they only sometimes match (T=20 F=28), so on 28 executions receive stays false and the input falls into the forwarding branches (L1227-1234, L1286), never evaluating the blocker's false side — loser blocked=8/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer of intercepted integer/memory comparisons (the address-equality checks behind HasUnicastAddress and the header field parses) and substitutes the matching operand directly into the input at the right offset, synthesizing the exact destination-address / header bytes the node expects; naive lacks CMP interception and must hit those multi-byte constants by blind havoc, which it rarely completes.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of `if (!aIsReassembled)` at ip6.cpp:1267, inside the receive path.
The globally blocked side is the false branch.
The blocker line 1267 is only reached when receive is true, since it is nested under `if (receive)` at L1257.
receive is set true when the destination matches a configured unicast address at L1223.
The winner always takes the unicast-address-match side at L1223 (T=70 F=0) while the loser diverges (T=20 F=28).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1223  T=70 F=0  T=20 F=28  if (Get<ThreadNetif>().HasUnicastAddress(header.GetDestin...

evidence_trail.claim: Loser inputs that miss the unicast match fall into the forwarding branches L1227-1234 (loser-only coverage).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1227  T=0 F=0  T=2 F=26  else if (!header.GetDestination().IsLinkLocal())

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog resolves the branch on all 10 trials while naive blocks on 8, with cmplog being the I2S winner over naive.
The naive loser blocks the branch on 8 of 10 trials.
Winner-resolving seeds share a concentrated structured IPv6 header prefix.
Loser-blocking seeds carry a different prefix with long 44 / 30 byte runs, not the winner's structured header.
W and L diverge sharply at the early header offsets, with W concentrated on header bytes the L seeds lack.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator and must hit constants by blind havoc.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 13337 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0a1686eddd03593d` and observed exactly 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -, ParentInfo-only) at depth 18 of the 19-level ancestor chain; under the current LibAFL build that nameless row is the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find, exclusive to cmplog/vpc in the canonical set.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where reaching the `!aIsReassembled` false side does NOT require any multi-byte address/header constant match (e.g. receive is forced true unconditionally) yet cmplog still resolves it on all trials while naive blocks — that would show the divergence is not driven by I2S substitution of a comparison operand.

weakest_evidence_point: The link from the byte-diff header prefix to the specific HasUnicastAddress comparison is inferred from source structure plus per-role branch counts, not from a logged I2SRandReplace operand at a named offset.
